description:
Edits an existing file by replacing specific strings with new ones. Supports both single and batch edits.

Usage:
- oldString must match exactly (including whitespace and indentation)
- read output prefixes every line with a line number and a tab — that prefix is NOT part of the file; strip it before using line content in oldString/newString
- If oldString appears multiple times, provide more surrounding context to make it unique
- Set replaceAll=true to replace every occurrence
- Read the file first to understand its current content before editing
- For multiple edits to the same file, use the 'edits' array to apply all changes in one call
- When using 'edits', do NOT provide top-level oldString/newString
- Edits are applied sequentially, so later edits see the result of earlier ones

filePath.description: Absolute or relative path to the file to modify

oldString.description: The exact text to find and replace (for single edit mode)

newString.description: The text to replace it with (for single edit mode)

replaceAll.description: Replace all occurrences (default: false, for single edit mode)

edits.description: Array of edit operations to apply sequentially to the same file (batch edit mode)

oldString.description: The exact text to find and replace

newString.description: The text to replace it with
